blocks:
Ayahs

نٓ ۚ وَٱلْقَلَمِ وَمَا يَسْطُرُونَ (۱)
Nun. By the Pen and what they write: (1)
مَآ أَنتَ بِنِعْمَةِ رَبِّكَ بِمَجْنُونٍ (۲)
by your Lord’s blessing, you are not, crazy, (2)
وَإِنَّ لَكَ لَأَجْرًا غَيْرَ مَمْنُونٍ (۳)
and yours indeed will be an everlasting reward, (3)
وَإِنَّكَ لَعَلَىٰ خُلُقٍ عَظِيمٍ (٤)
and indeed you possess a great character. (4)

Key Points

If Mamnūn («ممنون») is derived from Mann («مَنّ») meaning 'to cut off,' it means an uninterrupted reward. And if it is derived from Minna («منّت») (favor/obligation), it means that divine favors are without obligation. Apparently, the first meaning is intended, because showing forth a blessing on the part of Allah is not a fault; rather, it becomes an incentive for gratitude and obedience to Him. (1)

The role of the pen is greater than the role of the tongue, the sword, the Dirham and Dinar, fame, or children. This is because the pen transmits the experience of centuries and develops culture. Individuals can be put to sleep or awakened by the pen. A nation can be honored or humiliated by the pen. The pen is a silent scream. The pen is an official document. The pen is the reporter of history. Swearing by the pen is a sign of culture and civilization. Reliance on the pen is reliance on documentation and reasoning. Connection with the pen is connection with knowledge. Among all sounds, three sounds are privileged: the sound of the scholars' pen, the sound of the Mujahideen's footsteps, and the sound of the spinners' wheel. Yes, a nation is honored whose knowledge, power, and economy are dynamic. And if we want to mention these three sounds today, we must say: the sound of the printing press, the artillery, and the factory—meaning cultural, military, and economic power. Pens and writings are the guardians of knowledge. In a Hadith, we read: «قیّدوا العلم بالكتابةِ» (2)

The One who swears by the pen and writing is He who never wrote a single line in His entire life: «لا تخطّه بیمینك» (3). In the words of Hafez:

My beloved, who neither went to school nor wrote a line, Taught a hundred teachers lessons with a glance.

Do not be surprised by the attribution of madness to prominent individuals. The Quran states: «كذلك ما اتى الّذین من قبلهم من رسول الاّ قالوا ساحر او مجنون» (4) No messenger came to those before them except that they said: He is a sorcerer or mad.

Do not regard divine rewards lightly. «أجر كبیر» (5), «أجر عظیم» (6), «أجر كریم» (7), «أجر غیر ممنون» (8), «أجراً حسنا» (9), «أجره على اللّه» (10).

Ethics in Islam

Among the attributes and characteristics of the Prophet, the Quran emphasizes his ethics and calls it Khuluqin 'Azeem (a great character). On this occasion, we quote and translate the sayings of some religious leaders on this topic from the book Mizan al-Hikmah, Chapter on Character.

Imam Ali عليه السلام said: «رُبّ عزیز اذلّه خلقه و ذلیل اعزّه خلقه» How many honored people are humiliated by their bad character, and how many humiliated people are honored by their good character.

Imam Hassan عليه السلام said: «حُسن الخلق رأس كلّ بِرّ» Good character is the head of every good deed.

The Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: «انّما تفسیر حسن الخلق ان اصاب الدنیا یرضى و انّ لم یصبه لم یسخط» The interpretation of good character is that if one attains the world, he is pleased, and if he does not attain it, he does not become angry.

Imam Ali عليه السلام said: «ان العبد المسلم یبلغ بحسن خلقه درجة الصائم القائم» Indeed, the Muslim servant reaches the rank of the fasting and praying person through his good character.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The noble traits of character (Makārim al-Akhlāq) are ten things: certainty, contentment, patience, gratitude, forbearance, good character, generosity, zeal/honor (Ghayrah), courage, and chivalry (Javānmardī).

Imam Ali عليه السلام said: «من مكارم الاخلاق، ان تصل من قطعك و تعطى من حرمك و تعفوا من ظلمك» Among the noble traits of character is that you connect with him who cuts you off, you give to him who deprives you, and you forgive him who wrongs you.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «الخلق الحسن یمیت الخطیئة كما تمیت الشمس الخبیث» Good character kills sin just as the sun kills filth.

He also said: «حسن الخلق یثبت المودّة» Good character establishes affection.

The Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: «خصلتان لا یجتمعان فى المؤمن، البخل و سوء الخلق» Two qualities do not gather in a believer: stinginess and bad character.

It was said to the Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: Such-and-such a woman is devoted to worship but has a bad character and hurts her neighbor with her tongue. The Prophet said: «لا خیر فیها هى من اهل النار» There is no good in her; she is among the people of the Fire.

It is narrated in traditions that when the Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله buried Sa'd ibn Mu'adh, he said: Due to his bad temper at home, he was subjected to the pressure of the grave.

Great Character (Khuluqin 'Azeem)

The word Khuluq refers to attributes that are intertwined with a person's nature and disposition, and it does not refer to seasonal or temporary behaviors.

Various interpretations have been given regarding Khuluqin 'Azeem, including:

A) Aisha said: The Prophet's character encompasses the first 10 verses of Surah Al-Mu'minun, and there is no praise higher than this praise.

B) The intent is adopting Islamic ethics and a noble disposition.

C) The intent is patience upon the truth and managing affairs according to the dictates of reason.

D) Some lexicography books have taken Khuluq to mean religion and creed, just as we read in a Hadith from Imam Baqir عليه السلام that he said: The intent of Khuluqin 'Azeem is the religion of Islam. (11)

E) The intent is noble interaction with opponents, just as Allah commanded him to this method: «خذ العفو و امر بالعروف و اعرض عن الجاهلین» (12)

F) The intent is noble traits of character (Makārim al-Akhlāq), as it is narrated from the Prophet that he said: "I was only sent to perfect the noble traits of character," and he said: «ادّبنى ربّى فاحسن تادیبى» (13) My Lord educated me, and how excellent was His education.

G) Aisha said regarding the Prophet's character: «كان خلقه القرآن» (14) The Prophet's character was the embodiment of the Quran.

The late Allamah Tabataba'i, in Tafsir al-Mizan (Vol. 6, p. 183), narrated about 27 pages of traditions regarding the ethics, customs, and manners of the life of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, some of which we list briefly:

  1. The Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله would mend his own shoes.
  2. He would patch his own clothes.
  3. He would milk the sheep himself.
  4. He would eat with slaves.
  5. He would sit on the ground.
  6. He would ride a donkey.
  7. Shyness did not prevent him from obtaining his necessities from the market.
  8. He would shake hands with the rich and the poor and would not withdraw his hand until the other person withdrew theirs.
  9. He would greet everyone he met, whether old or young.
  10. If something was offered to him, he would not belittle it, even if it was a rotten date.
  11. He was frugal, noble in disposition, and pleasant in companionship.
  12. He always had a smile on his lips without laughing loudly.
  13. He always appeared sorrowful without having a frown on his face.
  14. He was always humble without showing any sign of abasement.
  15. He was generous without being extravagant.
  16. He was very tender-hearted and kind.
  17. He never stretched out the hand of greed toward anything.
  18. When leaving the house, he would look at himself in the mirror, comb his hair, and sometimes do this in front of water.
  19. He never stretched out his feet in front of others.
  20. He always chose the more difficult of two tasks.
  21. He never sought revenge for an injustice done to him, unless the sacred limits of Allah were violated, in which case he would become angry due to the violation of sanctity.
  22. He never ate while reclining.
  23. No person ever asked him for something and received a 'no,' and he never rejected the need of those who sought help.
  24. His prayer, despite being complete, was light, and his sermon was short.
  25. People recognized him by the pleasant scent that reached them from him.
  26. When he had a guest at home, he was the first to start eating and the last to stop eating, so that the guests could eat comfortably.
  27. At the dining cloth, he always ate from the food directly in front of him.
  28. He would drink water in three breaths.
  29. He would not give, take, or eat anything except with his right hand.
  30. When he prayed, he prayed three times, and when he spoke, he did not repeat his words.
  31. If he sought permission to enter people's homes, he would repeat it three times.
  32. His speech was clear, such that every listener understood it.
  33. He divided his gaze among the people present in his company.
  34. Whenever he spoke with people, he would smile while talking.

1) Tafsir Rahnamā. 2) Bihār al-Anwār, Vol. 2, p. 52. 3) Al-Ankabut, 48. 4) Adh-Dhariyat, 52. 5) Hud, 11. 6) Al-i Imran, 173. 7) Ya Sin, 11. 8) Fussilat, 8. 9) Al-Qalam, 4. 10) An-Nisa, 10. 11) Tafsir Nur ath-Thaqalayn 12) Al-A'raf, 199. 13) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan 14) Sunan an-Nabi, Allamah Tabataba'i, p. 56.

Messages

  1. Swearing by the pen instead of swearing by speech is an encouragement for literacy and the status of scholarship. (Fighting illiteracy and encouraging reading and writing are priority programs in Islam.) («والقلم و ما یسطرون»)

  2. The infallibility and immunity of the Prophet are under the shadow of divine grace. «ما انت بنعمة ربّك بمجنون»

  3. What makes a person steadfast against the pressure of slander is attention to divine grace and reward. «انّ لكَ لاجراً غیر ممنون»

  4. If we overlook the rewards of people, we attain the lasting rewards of Allah. «َلاجراً غیر ممنون»

  5. The eternal reward is for the one whose character is great. «لأجراً غیر ممنون... . لعلى خلق عظیم»

  6. Mastery over a great character is concurrent with mastery over the straight path. «انّك لعلى خلق عظیم» (And in another place, He says: «انّك لمن المرسلین على صراط مستقیم» (15))

  7. The Prophet's perfections are intertwined with him, and he has mastery over them. «لعلى خلق عظیم»

  8. It is necessary to defend religious figures who are subjected to slander and humiliation. «ما انت بنعمة ربّك بمجنون - انّك لعلى خلق عظیم»

15) Ya Sin, 3 - 4.

Ayahs

فَسَتُبْصِرُ وَيُبْصِرُونَ (٥)
You will see and they will see, (5)
بِأَييِّكُمُ ٱلْمَفْتُونُ (٦)
which one of you is crazy. (6)
إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَن ضَلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِٱلْمُهْتَدِينَ (۷)
Indeed your Lord knows best those who stray from His way, and He knows best those who are guided. (7)

Key Points

It is narrated in traditions (Riwāyāt) that when the Quraysh saw that the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله preferred Ali عليه السلام over others, they said: "Muhammad has become infatuated (maftūn) with him," upon which these verses were revealed. (16) We read a similar tone in Surah Al-Qamar: «سیعلمون من الكذّاب الاشر» (17) Tomorrow they will know who the liar and the self-indulgent one is. These verses also contain a type of glad tidings regarding the victory of the Prophet of Islam over the disbelievers (kuffār).

16) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān. 17) Al-Qamar, 26.

Messages

1- Allah consoles His Prophet against the enemies. «فستبصر و یبصرون»

2- The future clarifies the realities. «فستبصر و یبصرون»

3- Disbelievers (kuffār) who knowingly and deliberately reject the clear proofs of the Prophets deserve the title of 'madman' (majnūn). «بایّكم المفتون» (Just as conscious faith is a sign of wisdom.)

4- Attributing misguidance to others or claiming guidance for oneself is ineffective. It is Allah Who knows the truly misguided and the truly guided. «هو اعلم بمن ضلّ عن سبیله و هو اعلم بالمهتدین»

Ayahs

فَلَا تُطِعِ ٱلْمُكَذِّبِينَ (۸)
So do not obey the deniers, (8)
وَدُّوا۟ لَوْ تُدْهِنُ فَيُدْهِنُونَ (۹)
who are eager that you should be flexible, so that they [too] may be flexible [towards you]. (9)
وَلَا تُطِعْ كُلَّ حَلَّافٍ مَّهِينٍ (۱٠)
And do not obey any vile swearer, (10)
هَمَّازٍ مَّشَّآءٍۭ بِنَمِيمٍ (۱۱)
scandal-monger, talebearer, (11)
مَّنَّاعٍ لِّلْخَيْرِ مُعْتَدٍ أَثِيمٍ (۱۲)
hinderer of all good, sinful transgressor, (12)
عُتُلٍّۭ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ زَنِيمٍ (۱۳)
callous and, on top of that, baseborn (13)
أَن كَانَ ذَا مَالٍ وَبَنِينَ (۱٤)
—[who behaves in such a manner only] because he has wealth and children. (14)
إِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِ ءَايَـٰتُنَا قَالَ أَسَـٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۱٥)
When Our signs are recited to him, he says, ‘Myths of the ancients!’ (15)
سَنَسِمُهُۥ عَلَى ٱلْخُرْطُومِ (۱٦)
Soon We shall brand him on his snout. (16)

Key Points

“Tudhin” (تدهن) is derived from “Duhn” (دُهن), meaning oil, and the intent here is oiling, compromise, and flexibility. “Hammāz” (هَمّاز) is derived from “Hamz” (هَمز), meaning fault-finder, and is synonymous with the word “Ayyāb” (عیّاب). “Mashshā’ bi-Namīm” (مَشّاء بنَمیم) means one who strives greatly for gossip and slander (namīmah). The intent of “Mannā‘ lil-Khayr” (مَنّاع للخیر) is perhaps stinginess with wealth, because the Quran has used the word ‘khayr’ (good) regarding wealth. (18) “Zanīm” (زَنیم) refers to someone whose lineage is unclear and who is not attributed to any tribe (illegitimate child). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: “‘Utull’ (عُتلّ) is great disbelief (kufr), and ‘Zanīm’ is someone who has greed and voracity in his disbelief. (19)

The Quran has repeatedly forbidden the Messenger of Allah from following the deviants using the phrases “lā tuṭi‘" (لا تطع) (20) and “lā tattabi‘" (لا تتّبع). (21)

To save the people, introduce the enemy with all its dimensions. These verses mention about ten characteristics, avoidance of which is necessary due to just one of them, let alone if all these characteristics are present in a group or an individual.

The leader of the society, who is obeyed and accepted by the people, must be the source of vitality, hope, unity, and piety. Characteristics such as fault-finding, slander, stinginess, transgression, and harshness—each of which is a factor leading to discouragement and division—are forbidden for the leader. In these verses, obedience to the possessors of these characteristics is prohibited.

The verses of the Quran have repeatedly been subjected to various accusations, with expressions such as “asāṭīr al-awwalīn” (اساطیر الاولین) (legends of the ancients), “aḍghāthu aḥlām” (اضغاث احلام) (22) (confused dreams), “am yaqūlūna iftarāh” (ام یقولون افتراه) (23) (lies attributed to God), “laqulnā mithla hādhā” (لقلنا مثل هذا) (24) (words that we too could say the like of), “yu‘allimuhu bashar” (یعلّمه بشر) (25) (a human taught him these words), and “a‘ānahu ‘alayhi qawm” (اعانه علیه قوم) (26) (a group is helping him behind the scenes).

We read in narrations: Allah told Prophet Shu‘ayb: I will afflict a population of one hundred thousand people with My wrath, even though forty thousand of them are wicked and sixty thousand are righteous. This is because the righteous lack religious zeal and compromise (mudāhanah) with the sinners. (27)

It should not be hidden that what is criticized is compromise from a position of weakness, which is called mudāhanah (compromise/flattery), but yielding from a position of strength, which is called mudārāh (tolerance/gentleness), is permissible, like a father who walks slowly out of consideration for his child.

“Hammāz, Mannā‘, Mu‘tad, and Athīm” (همّاز، منّاع، معتد و اثیم) are characteristics of the disbelievers (kuffār). Whenever a Muslim possesses these characteristics, he has drawn close to the disbelievers.

18) «ان ترك خیراً الوصیة» (If he leaves behind wealth, he should make a will). 19) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 20) Kahf, 26; Furqan, 52; Ahzab, 1 and 48; Qalam, 8 and 10; Insan, 24. 21) Ma’idah, 48 and 49; An‘am, 150; A‘raf, 142; Sad, 26; Shura, 15; Jathiyah, 18. 22) Anbiya, 5. 23) Ahqaf, 8. 24) Anfal, 31. 25) Nahl, 103. 26) Furqan, 4. 27) Tafsir Atyab al-Bayan.

Messages

1- In gratitude for Allah’s blessings, avoid the deviants. «انّك لعلى خلق عظیم فلا تطع المكذّبین»

2- Good character is accompanied by repulsion and avoidance of the deviants. «لعلى خلق عظیم فلا تطع المكذّبین» (Indeed, the meaning of good character is not compromise with corrupt individuals.)

3- Even the Prophets need divine reminder. «فلا تطع المكذّبین»

4- The oaths of opponents hold no credibility, and the more they swear, the less credible they become. «لا تطع كلّ حلاف»

5- In an Islamic society, individuals of unclear lineage and baseness (“mahīn”), fault-finders (“namīm”), and those whose nature includes stinginess, transgression, sin, and harshness, have no social, political, or managerial standing. «فلا تطع...»

6- Do not be heedless of the enemy’s plots. Enemies strive to draw you into compromise. «ودّوا لو تدهن...»

7- Do not grant the enemy concessions, even if they signal the green light for compromise by offering concessions. «ودّوا لو تدهن...»

8- Retreating from principles is the enemy’s desire. «ودّوا لو تدهن» (Leniency and laxity regarding principles are forbidden.)

9- Compromise with the enemy is tantamount to obeying him. And the intent of not obeying is precisely not compromising. «فلا تطع... ودّوا لو تدهن فیدهنون»

10- Inner baseness and the possession of external resources are two factors leading to discord (fitnah) and corruption. «مهین... ذا مال و بنین»

11- The possession of wealth and power by the disbelievers cannot be a reason for compromise. «لو تدهن... ان كان ذا مال و بنین»

12- The frontline opponents of the Prophets are the carefree capitalists. «ذا مال و بنین ... قال اساطیر الاولین»

13- Allah punishes the arrogant in such a way that its marks remain on their bodies. «سنسمه»

14- The punishment for the humiliator is humiliation. «سنسمه على الخرطوم»

Ayahs

إِنَّا بَلَوْنَـٰهُمْ كَمَا بَلَوْنَآ أَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ إِذْ أَقْسَمُوا۟ لَيَصْرِمُنَّهَا مُصْبِحِينَ (۱۷)
Indeed we have tested them just as We tested the People of the Garden when they vowed they would gather its fruit at dawn, (17)
وَلَا يَسْتَثْنُونَ (۱۸)
and they did not make any exception. (18)
فَطَافَ عَلَيْهَا طَآئِفٌ مِّن رَّبِّكَ وَهُمْ نَآئِمُونَ (۱۹)
Then, a visitation from your Lord visited it while they were asleep. (19)
فَأَصْبَحَتْ كَٱلصَّرِيمِ (۲٠)
So, by dawn it was like a harvested field. (20)
فَتَنَادَوْا۟ مُصْبِحِينَ (۲۱)
At dawn they called out to one another, (21)
أَنِ ٱغْدُوا۟ عَلَىٰ حَرْثِكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰرِمِينَ (۲۲)
‘Get off early to your field if you have to gather [the fruits].’ (22)

Key Points

The meaning of ṭā’if (طائف) is a blight or calamity that circled the garden, burned it, and turned it to ashes. Ṣarm (صرم) means cutting and harvesting fruit, and the meaning of ṣarīm (صریم) is the uprooting and cutting down of all trees by Divine wrath. Tanādaw (تنادوا) means the calling of individuals to gather in one place for consultation and deliberation.

Divine wrath sometimes reaches individuals, just as Pharaoh and and his army were drowned: «اغرقناه و جنوده» (28). Sometimes it reaches wealth, like in these verses where their garden was burned but they themselves were safe. And sometimes it reaches both individuals and wealth, like Qarun, who, along with his house and his wealth, sank into the earth: «فخسفنا به و بداره» (29).

In this Surah, stinginess is criticized twice: once in the verse «منّاع للخیر» (a hinderer of good) and once in the story of these verses.

The good traditions of ancestors must be continued, but in this story, the inheritors decided to abandon their father's path and deprive the poor.

Sin is a cause of deprivation. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: «انّ الرجل لیذنب الذنب فیدرء عنه الرزق» (30) (Indeed, a man commits a sin, and by that measure, sustenance is withheld from him). A man who commits a sin is deprived of Divine sustenance to that extent. As mentioned in a narration: sometimes a person is deprived of the night prayer (Salat al-Layl) because of sin (31).

The value of wealth is only to the extent that it does not corrupt one's thinking; otherwise, by the command of Allah, the Mosque of Dirar is destroyed, and the golden calf of the Samiri is burned: «لنحرقنّه ثمّ لننسفنّه فى الیم نفسا» (32).

The Story of the Burned Garden:

A gardener who used to give charity to the poor from the fruits of his garden every year passed away. The inheritors decided to deprive the poor. Only one of the inheritors objected, but the majority were stingy. To deprive the poor, they decided to go to the garden at dawn and harvest the fruits so that the poor would not become aware. When they went to the garden, they saw it as a flat expanse of ashes. They said: We have lost our way; this is not our garden. But another said: We deviated from the righteous path of our father, and we decided to deprive the poor, so we ourselves were deprived. The son who had opposed the stinginess from the beginning said: Did I not tell you that this idea was wrong? In any case, they witnessed the Divine wrath, became alert, and blamed one another.

28) Yunus, 73. 29) Qasas, 81. 30) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 31) Tafsir Maraghi. 32) Taha, 97.

Messages

1- Trial (testing) is one of the Divine traditions. «انّابلوناكم كما بلونا»

2- The Quran explains various examples of Divine testing so that we may become familiar with the method of Allah’s trial. «كما بلونا اصحاب الجنة»

3- Plans that lead to the deprivation of the poor are unsuccessful. «اقسموا...و لا یستثنون... فاصبحت كالصریم»

4- Not every oath is valuable. «اقسموا لیصرمنّها مصبحین»

5- Divine wrath is not exclusive to the Hereafter; rather, it sometimes occurs in this world and very quickly. «فطاف علیها طائف من رتب... فاصبحت كالصریم»

6- Punishment and retribution are among the affairs of Lordship (Rububiyyah) and have an educational aspect. «طائف من ربّك»

7- No one can preempt Allah. (They rose early in the morning to harvest the fruits, but Allah burned the garden at dawn.) «و هم نائمون»

8- Punishment and crime must be proportionate. Because they deprived the poor, they themselves were deprived. «لا یستثنون... فاصبحت كالصریم»

9- Human thought and planning are insignificant compared to the planning of Allah. (Here, the inheritors made one decision, but Allah willed another.) «اقسموا لیصرمنّها مصبحین... فاصبحت كالصریم»

10- Allah is the Protector of the poor. Although the poor were unaware of the inheritors' decision, Allah burned the garden in support of the poor and to punish the stingy inheritors. «لا یستثنون... فاصبحت كالصریم»

11- Human successes are not confined to his calculations. In this story, the calculations were one thing, but the result was something else. «لیصرمنّها مصبحین... فاصبحت كالصریم»

12- A bad intention, if accompanied by planning and preliminary actions, incurs punishment. «و لا یستثنون... فاصبحت كالصریم»

13- Wealth from which the deprived do not benefit is better off non-existent. «فاصبحت كالصریم»

14- Greed and stinginess do not lead to increased capital or success. «لا یستثنون... فاصبحت كالصریم»

Ayahs

فَٱنطَلَقُوا۟ وَهُمْ يَتَخَـٰفَتُونَ (۲۳)
So off they went, murmuring to one another: (23)
أَن لَّا يَدْخُلَنَّهَا ٱلْيَوْمَ عَلَيْكُم مِّسْكِينٌ (۲٤)
‘Today no needy man shall come to you in it.’ (24)
وَغَدَوْا۟ عَلَىٰ حَرْدٍ قَـٰدِرِينَ (۲٥)
And they set out at early morning, [considering themselves] able to deprive [the poor of its fruit]. (25)
فَلَمَّا رَأَوْهَا قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا لَضَآلُّونَ (۲٦)
But when they saw it, they said, ‘We have indeed lost our way!’ (26)
بَلْ نَحْنُ مَحْرُومُونَ (۲۷)
‘No, it is we who have been deprived!’ (27)
قَالَ أَوْسَطُهُمْ أَلَمْ أَقُل لَّكُمْ لَوْلَا تُسَبِّحُونَ (۲۸)
The most upright among them said, ‘Did I not tell you, ‘‘Why do you not glorify [Allah]?’’ ’ (28)
قَالُوا۟ سُبْحَـٰنَ رَبِّنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا ظَـٰلِمِينَ (۲۹)
They said, ‘Immaculate is our Lord! We have indeed been wrongdoers!’ (29)
فَأَقْبَلَ بَعْضُهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ يَتَلَـٰوَمُونَ (۳٠)
Then they turned to one another, blaming each other. (30)
قَالُوا۟ يَـٰوَيْلَنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا طَـٰغِينَ (۳۱)
They said, ‘Woe to us! Indeed, we have been rebellious. (31)
عَسَىٰ رَبُّنَآ أَن يُبْدِلَنَا خَيْرًا مِّنْهَآ إِنَّآ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا رَٰغِبُونَ (۳۲)
Maybe our Lord will give us a better one in its place. Indeed we earnestly beseech our Lord.’ (32)
كَذَٰلِكَ ٱلْعَذَابُ ۖ وَلَعَذَابُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ أَكْبَرُ ۚ لَوْ كَانُوا۟ يَعْلَمُونَ (۳۳)
Such was their punishment; and the punishment of the Hereafter is surely greater, had they known. (33)

Key Points

“Hardi” means stinginess and preventing others. «غدوا على حرد» means the owners of the garden set out with the intention of depriving the poor.

Greed and stinginess make a person hard-hearted, to the extent that he does not show mercy to the needy and the poor. «لا یدخلنّها الیوم علیكم مسكین»

“Awsat” refers to someone who is moderate and far from extremism and negligence.(33)

Attending to the needy is not merely recommended (Mustahabb), but obligatory (Wajib), because Allah does not punish anyone for abandoning a recommended act. In this Ayah, depriving the needy caused the garden to burn in this world, and in the Hereafter, the inhabitants of Hell know the reason for their damnation is their disregard for the deprived. «ما سلككم فى سقر... لم نك نطعم المسكین»(34)

Attending to the poor is not exclusive to the believing poor. In this story, there is no mention of the faith or disbelief of the poor, but rather the stinginess and greed of some and the hunger and poverty of others.

Sometimes, in order to change thought and culture, an individual or a group of wrongdoers must be exposed in society so that they become a lesson for others. Allah, Who is «اظهر الجمیل و ستر القبیح» (He Who manifests the beautiful and conceals the ugly), unveils the matter, and the story of the garden that burned becomes a historical sign.

In supplication and conversation with Allah, we should use the word “Rabbana” frequently. In these verses, this word is repeated several times. «ربّنا انّا كنّا ظالمین... عسى ربّنا ان یبدلنا... اِنّا الى ربّنا راغبون»

When Allah’s wrath comes, it burns everyone. Even though one of the heirs opposed depriving the poor, his share also burned.

Examples of worldly punishment include:

A) Removal of blessing. B) Domination by the wicked. C) Removal of divine success (Tawfiq). D) Removal of security and peace.

But the punishment of the Hereafter is severe, painful, great, and immense. «و لعذاب الآخرة اكبر لو كانوا یعلمون»

Messages

1- The greater the evil intention, the greater the danger. «لا یدخلّنها» (They said: Let none of the poor enter the garden, and Allah burned the entire garden.)

2- Greed and stinginess sometimes become so severe that a person is not willing to benefit even a single poor person. «مسكین» is used in the singular form.

3- The greedy spirit «لا یستثنون», the secret decision «یتخافتون», and the powerful action «قادرین», none of them succeed. (بل نحن محرومون)

4- One of the benefits of bitter events is self-reflection. «فلما رأوها قالوا انّا لضالّون»

5- If we deprive others in one respect, we are deprived in several respects. «بل نحن محرومون» (Deprived of fruit, deprived of Divine reward, deprived of the prayers of the poor, deprived of the satisfaction of the father's soul, deprived of social honor.)

6- Speak the truth, even if you are in the minority. «قال اوسطهم الم اقل لكم...»

7- Moderation and wisdom lead to helping the needy and prevent greed and stinginess. «قال اوسطهم»

8- In the method of propagation, utilize opportunities. Those who were not willing to listen to the truth yesterday are willing to listen today. «قال اوسطهم الم اقل لكم...»

9- In analyzing and examining misfortunes, we should confess our own shortcomings and declare Allah free from imperfection. «سبحان ربّنا انّا كنّا ظالمین»

10- Compensate today for what you did not do yesterday. «لولا تسبّحون - قالوا سبحان ربّنا»

11- The root of deprivation from Divine favors is man's oppression of himself. «بل نحن محرومون... انّا كنّا ظالمین»

12- If unity is not based on piety, it eventually turns into discord. (The brothers who were previously united in depriving others, today blame each other and shift the guilt onto one another.) «فاقبل بعضهم على بعض یتلاومون»

13- Not giving others their right is oppression against oneself and transgression against others. «انّا كنّا ظالمین - انّا كنّا طاغین»

14- Before Allah, we should confess more fully to the weakness and ugliness of our actions. «انّا كنّا ظالمین - انّا كنّا طاغین» (As Ali عليه السلام says in Du'a Kumayl: «معتذراً نادماً، منكسراً مستقیلاً، مستغفراً منیباً مقراً مذعناً معترفاً»)

15- When losing worldly possessions, be hopeful of Allah’s grace and mercy that He will give you something better than them. «عسى ربّنا ان یبدلنا خیراً منها»

16- Allah’s Hand is open for provision and compensation. Sinners should not despair and should not consider themselves defeated forever. «یبدلنا خیراً منها»

17- It is never too late to return to Allah. «انّا الى ربّنا راغبون» (The owners of the garden, upon seeing the burned garden, woke up from the sleep of negligence, repented, and turned toward Allah.)

18- Breaking the heart of the poor has worldly and otherworldly punishment. «كذلك العذاب و لعذاب الآخرة»

19- Belief in the punishment and torment of the Day of Judgment prevents stinginess, transgression, and oppression. «ولعذاب الآخرة اكبر لو كانوا یعلمون»

(33) Mufradat Raghib. (34) Al-Muddaththir, 42 and 44.

Ayahs

إِنَّ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ جَنَّـٰتِ ٱلنَّعِيمِ (۳٤)
Indeed for the Godwary there will be gardens of bliss near their Lord. (34)
أَفَنَجْعَلُ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ كَٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ (۳٥)
Shall We treat those who submit [to Us] as [We treat] the guilty? (35)
مَا لَكُمْ كَيْفَ تَحْكُمُونَ (۳٦)
What is the matter with you? How do you judge! (36)
أَمْ لَكُمْ كِتَـٰبٌ فِيهِ تَدْرُسُونَ (۳۷)
Do you possess a scripture in which you read (37)
إِنَّ لَكُمْ فِيهِ لَمَا تَخَيَّرُونَ (۳۸)
that you shall have in it whatever you choose? (38)
أَمْ لَكُمْ أَيْمَـٰنٌ عَلَيْنَا بَـٰلِغَةٌ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۙ إِنَّ لَكُمْ لَمَا تَحْكُمُونَ (۳۹)
Do you have a pledge binding on Us until the Day of Resurrection, that you shall indeed have whatever you decide? (39)
سَلْهُمْ أَيُّهُم بِذَٰلِكَ زَعِيمٌ (٤٠)
Ask them, which of them will aver [any of] that! (40)
أَمْ لَهُمْ شُرَكَآءُ فَلْيَأْتُوا۟ بِشُرَكَآئِهِمْ إِن كَانُوا۟ صَـٰدِقِينَ (٤۱)
Do they have any ‘partners’ [that they claim for Allah]? Then let them produce their partners, if they are truthful. (41)

Key Points

A group used to say: There is no Resurrection, and even if there is, we will be in complete comfort there too. «و ما اظنّ الساعة قائمة و لئن رجعت الى ربّى اَنّ لى عنده للحسنى»(35) These verses are the answer to that false notion.

35) Fussilat, 50.

Messages

1- In education and guidance, encouragement must accompany warning. «ولعذاب الآخرة اكبر - انّ للمتّقین»

2- The gardens of Paradise are full of blessings, contrary to worldly gardens which have troubles and afflictions alongside their blessings. (جنات النعیم)

3- Punishment and reward are based on justice. «افنجعل المسلمین كالمجرمین»

4- Every sound conscience and nature approves of justice. «افنجعل المسلمین كالمجرمین»

5- Reliance and argumentation must be either upon a Divine Book, which is proof: «ام لكم كتاب», or upon reason: «ما لكم كیف تحكمون», or based on commitment and covenant: «ام لكم ایمان بالغة»

6- Divine Books are not subject to the unrestrained desires of humans. «ام لكم كتاب... انّ لكم فیه لما تخیّرون» (It has not been stated in any Divine Book that whatever you wish will come to pass.)

7- Allah has given no commitment that the truth is whatever you decree. «ام لكم ایمان... انّ لكم لما تحكمون»

Ayahs

يَوْمَ يُكْشَفُ عَن سَاقٍ وَيُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ فَلَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ (٤۲)
The day when the catastrophe occurs and they are summoned to prostrate themselves, they will not be able [to do it]. (42)
خَـٰشِعَةً أَبْصَـٰرُهُمْ تَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ ۖ وَقَدْ كَانُوا۟ يُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ وَهُمْ سَـٰلِمُونَ (٤۳)
With a humbled look [in their eyes], they will be overcast by abasement. Certainly, they were summoned to prostrate themselves while they were yet sound. (43)

Key Points

The meaning of "یُكشف عن ساق" (yukhshafu 'an sāq) is the same idiom we use in Persian: "the knife has reached the bone" (meaning extreme difficulty/crisis). In a Hadith from Imam Sadiq علیه السلام, we read: On that Day, mouths will be sealed, fear will encompass the entire being, eyes will stare blankly, and souls will reach the throats. (36) In Surah Al-Furqan, Ayah 60, we read: «و اذا قیل لهم اسجدوا للرحمن قالوا و ما الرحمن انسجد لما تأمرنا» Whenever it is said to them: Prostrate before the God of Mercy (Ar-Rahman), they say: Who is Ar-Rahman? Should we prostrate to what you command us? The Ayah under discussion, which states: «كانوا یدعون الى السجود» (They were called to prostration), refers to that Ayah. The meaning of the call to prostration in this world, «كانوا یدعون الى السجود و هم سالمون» (They were called to prostration while they were safe), is either the call of the Adhan (call to prayer) which invites people to prayer and prostration, or the verses (Ayat) that command worship, or the instructions whose implementation is a form of prostration, humility, and lowliness before Allah—not to mention that prostration is the peak of worship.

36) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The Day of Judgment is the day when hardships manifest. «یكشف عن ساق»

2- The Day of Judgment is the embodiment of this world. He who was not a person of prostration in this world will not have the ability to prostrate on the Day of Judgment. «یدعون الى السّجود فلا یستطیعون»

3- Those who do not utilize the opportunity of free will and health should await a Day when they will possess none of these opportunities. «یدعون الى السّجود و هم سالمون»

4- The criminal will have no face (courage) to lift his head and look up. «خاشعة ابصارهم»

5- The humiliation of the Day of Judgment is pervasive. «ترهقهم ذلّة»

6- He who does not accept voluntary humility before Allah will, on that Day, accept compulsory humility and disgrace. «خاشعة... ترهقهم ذلّة»

Ayahs

فَذَرْنِى وَمَن يُكَذِّبُ بِهَـٰذَا ٱلْحَدِيثِ ۖ سَنَسْتَدْرِجُهُم مِّنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (٤٤)
So leave Me with those who deny this discourse. We will draw them imperceptibly [into ruin], whence they do not know. (44)
وَأُمْلِى لَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ كَيْدِى مَتِينٌ (٤٥)
I will grant them respite, for My devising is indeed sure. (45)

Key Points

The word istidrāj (استدراج) means approaching degree by degree and step by step. The intent is that Allah gradually brings a group closer to the abyss of downfall in a way they do not perceive. There are many verses in the Quran stating that We grant respite to the sinners, and they should not imagine that We love them or have forgotten them. Imam Sadiq صلى الله علیه وآله said: If a sin is committed and the sinner remains in comfort and affluence, this is a sign of divine istidrāj leading to punishment.(37)

37) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan.

Messages

1- The deniers are dealing directly with Allah. «ذرنى و من یكذّب»

2- Allah consoles His Prophet. «ذرنى و من یكذّب»

3- Denying the Quran carries a very great punishment, because Allah says: Leave them to Me. «فذرنى و من یكذّب بهذا الحدیث»

4- The Quran is full of new and fresh discourse. «بهذا الحدیث» (Hadith = new discourse)

5- Do not judge hastily, and do not consider every comfort a sign of felicity. (Animals are taken to pasture and fattened, but their end is the slaughterhouse.) «سنستدرجهم من حیث لا یعلمون»

6- The greatest danger is for a person to be unaware of it and not seek a remedy. «من حیث لا یعلمون»

7- Allah grants respite (imhal) but does not neglect (ihmal). «سنستدرجهم... ان كیدى متین»

8- Divine respites are Allah's plan for the destruction of the disbelievers. «اُملى لهم انّ كیدى متین»

9- Human conspiracies are neutralized, but the Divine plan is firm. «انّ كیدى متین»

Ayahs

أَمْ تَسْـَٔلُهُمْ أَجْرًا فَهُم مِّن مَّغْرَمٍ مُّثْقَلُونَ (٤٦)
Do you ask them for a reward, so that they are weighed down with debt? (46)
أَمْ عِندَهُمُ ٱلْغَيْبُ فَهُمْ يَكْتُبُونَ (٤۷)
Do they possess [access to] the Unseen, so that they write it down? (47)
فَٱصْبِرْ لِحُكْمِ رَبِّكَ وَلَا تَكُن كَصَاحِبِ ٱلْحُوتِ إِذْ نَادَىٰ وَهُوَ مَكْظُومٌ (٤۸)
So submit patiently to the judgement of your Lord, and do not be like the Man of the Fish who called out as he choked with grief. (48)
لَّوْلَآ أَن تَدَٰرَكَهُۥ نِعْمَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ لَنُبِذَ بِٱلْعَرَآءِ وَهُوَ مَذْمُومٌ (٤۹)
Had it not been for a blessing that came to his rescue from his Lord, he would surely have been cast on the bare shore, being blameworthy. (49)
فَٱجْتَبَـٰهُ رَبُّهُۥ فَجَعَلَهُۥ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (٥٠)
So his Lord chose him and made him one of the righteous. (50)

Key Points

Maghram (مَغرم), derived from Gharāmah (غرامت), means a loss that befalls a person without him having committed a crime or betrayal. Muthqalūn (مُثقلون), derived from Thiqal (ثِقل), means heaviness, whether material or spiritual. Makẓūm (مَكظوم) refers to someone who is filled with grief and sorrow. Ayahs 46 and 47 of this Surah are similar to Ayahs 40 and 41 of Surah At-Tur. Allah has invited His Prophet to patience about twenty times in the Qur'an, because guiding people requires patience and steadfastness. In Surah Ash-Shu'ara, the motto of all the Prophets is that "We do not ask you people for any reward." «وما اسئلكم علیه من اجر»(38) The disbelievers were threatened in Ayah 45 with the phrase «انّ كیدى متین» (Indeed, My plan is firm). Therefore, Allah has a decree and a plan for them; you must be patient and do not rush to curse them. «فاصبر لحكم ربّك»

Question: What was meant by the "favor of the Lord" that Yunus (Jonah) received?

Answer: Both material favor was bestowed upon him, because he did not drown and the fish swallowed him, he remained alive in the belly of the fish, and then it cast him out. And also spiritual favor, which was the success (Tawfīq) of his apology and the acceptance of his repentance, because no repentance or obedience issues from a person except through Divine success (Tawfīq).

Question: Although Prophet Yunus was treated with kindness, why was he still cast out onto a barren land? As He says in Surah As-Saffat: «فنبذناه بالعراء و هو سقیم و انبتنا علیه شجرة من یقطین»(39) (Then We cast him onto the barren shore while he was sick, and We caused a plant of gourd to grow over him [until he recovered]).

Answer: Being cast onto the barren land accompanied by the cure of illness is a favor (Luṭf), but being cast out with eternal blame and reproach is wrath (Qahr).

38) Ash-Shu'ara, 109, 127, 145, 164, and 180. 39) As-Saffat, 145-146.

Messages

1- The Prophets did not ask people for a reward. «ام تسئلهم اجراً»

2- Naturally, people flee from a religious scholar whose goal is material gain. «فهم من مغرم مثقلون»

3- The patience and steadfastness that is commanded is that which is for the sake of Allah's decree, not out of stubbornness or undue obstinacy. «فاصبر لحكم ربّك»

4- The leader of the community must not abandon his nation under any circumstances. «لا تكن كصاحب الحوت»

5- Supplication (Du'a) and intimate prayer (Munājāt) are the basis for Divine provision/remedy (Tadāruk). «نادى ... تداركه»

6- The success (Tawfīq) of repentance is a Divine favor. «تداركه نعمة من ربّه»

7- Reproach, blame, and wandering in barren lands is the sole punishment for a single instance of impatience. «لنبذ بالعراء و هو مذموم»

8- A cry and lamentation is valuable only if it arises from within; otherwise, it is hypocrisy. «نادى و هو مكظوم»

9- Repentance and conversation with Allah is a path to receiving special favors. «اذ نادى و هو مكظوم... فاجتباه ربّه»

10- Instead of rejecting individuals for a single slip, we should remedy their situation (Tadāruk), remove their weaknesses, and then give them responsibility. «تداركه نعمة من ربّه... فاجتباه ربّه»

11- The selection of Prophets is solely in the hands of Allah. «فاجتباه ربّه»

Ayahs

وَإِن يَكَادُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَيُزْلِقُونَكَ بِأَبْصَـٰرِهِمْ لَمَّا سَمِعُوا۟ ٱلذِّكْرَ وَيَقُولُونَ إِنَّهُۥ لَمَجْنُونٌ (٥۱)
Indeed the faithless almost devour you with their eyes when they hear this Reminder, and they say, ‘He is indeed crazy.’ (51)
وَمَا هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكْرٌ لِّلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (٥۲)
Yet it is just a reminder for all the nations. (52)

Key Points

At the beginning of this Surah, the accusation of madness against the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله was alluded to, and in this Ayah, which is the end of the Surah, this attribution is explicitly stated. "و یقولون انّه لمجنون"

Liyuzliqunaka (لیزلقونك) is derived from the root zalq (زلق), meaning to slip or fall to the ground, and is a metaphor for destruction and annihilation. Ayah 51 expresses the intensity of the disbelievers' wrath, who, upon hearing the verses of the Quran, wish to destroy you with their eyes. The intent of this is either destruction through the evil eye (chashm zakhm) or it is a metaphor, just as we say, "So-and-so wanted to devour me with his gaze."

Hatred and malice compel a person toward contradiction. On the one hand, he considers the Quran magnificent, such that his envy seeks to destroy the Prophet, and on the other hand, he accuses him of incoherent speech and madness. The enemy fights with the hand, slanders with the tongue, and intends destruction with the eye.

The beginning of the Surah spoke of the pen and writing, and the end of the Surah speaks of the awakening of all mankind. Perhaps this is an indication that the key to the awakening of mankind lies in cultural tools.

The Evil Eye (Chashm Zakhm)

Regarding the evil eye, the Prophet said: "انّ العین حقّ و انّها تُدخل الجمل والثور التّنور" (The evil eye is real, and it is so effective that it can put a camel and an ox into the oven.) (40)

The evil eye does not necessarily have to come from an enemy. Sometimes a friend is astonished by a perfection possessed by his own friend, and thus we have a tradition (Hadith) that if you see something in your friend that astonishes you, remember Allah so that the affliction of the evil eye may be averted, and how many are those who have been destroyed and have died by means of the evil eye. (41)

Of course, the reality of the evil eye does not mean that we should harbor suspicion toward certain individuals and accuse them of having an evil eye, nor should we attribute our own shortcomings in various matters to the evil eye.

We read in narrations: charity (sadaqah), supplication (du'a), reciting Surahs An-Nas and Al-Falaq, and similar acts, can prevent the effect of the evil eye.

40) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 63, p. 17. 41) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 95, p. 127.

Messages

1- The enemy's malice is serious; one must be vigilant. "و ان یكاد الّذین كفروا لیزلقونك"

2- The Quran unveils the conspiracies of the enemy. "و ان یكادالّذین كفروا"

3- The sharp edge of the enemy's attack is directed at the leader of the Islamic society. "لیزلقونك"

4- The evil eye is a reality. "لیزلقونك بابصارهم"

5- One must defend against accusations leveled against the Divine Saints (Awliya' Allah). In response to "یقولون انه لمجنون" (They say he is surely mad), Allah said: "ما انت بنعمة ربّك بمجنون" (You are not, by the grace of your Lord, mad).

6- The Quran is a means of removing heedlessness. "ان هو الا ذكر"

7- The Quran is not restricted to a specific place or time; it is a universal and eternal book for all nations. "ذكر للعالمین"
